title: The Deposition Mosaic; St Mark's Basilica
tags: life christ deposition mosaic post renaissance byzantine gothic style sculpture statues roof line front west facade saint st mark 馬可 聖馬可 patriarchal cathedral basilica san marco 聖馬爾谷聖殿宗主教座堂 cattedrale patriarcale sanmarco 聖堂 veneto venice 威尼斯 venezia exterior 教堂 church 教會 主教座堂 christian catholic italia 意大利 italy olympus em1
description: The Deposition, is the first of the four mosaics depicting scenes from the Life of Christ.  The Life of Christ mosaics are all post-Renaissance replacements of earlier mosaics.  Above the mosaic, statues and sculptures fill the roof line that is mostly in a Gothic style.  Saint Marks Basilica, Venice; March 2017

title: Upper Facade; St Mark's Basilica
tags: life christ descent limbo mosaic post renaissance byzantine gothic style sculpture statues roof line front west facade saint st mark 馬可 聖馬可 patriarchal cathedral basilica san marco 聖馬爾谷聖殿宗主教座堂 cattedrale patriarcale sanmarco 聖堂 veneto venice 威尼斯 venezia exterior 教堂 church 教會 主教座堂 christian catholic italia 意大利 italy olympus em1
description: A view of the upper facade, just right of center.  The mosaic here is the third of the four Life of Christ mosaics, Descent into Limbo.  The Life of Christ mosaics are all post-Renaissance replacements of earlier mosaics.  Above the mosaic, statues and sculptures fill the roof line that is mostly in a Gothic style.  'Sheltered' under the turrets hare are the Apostle Evangelists St John (left) and St Luke (right).  Saint Marks Basilica, Venice; March 2017

title: Skittles
tags: city longexposure travel venice sky urban blackandwhite bw italy cloud holiday motion blur building water lines architecture clouds island canal blackwhite movement dock europe italia blurred it basin line slowshutter ita gondola vignetting vignette grandcanal gondolas sangiorgiomaggiore neutraldensity a99 saintmarkbasin 16stop formatthitech sonya99 slta99 sonyslta99 sonyalphaslta99 aaronyeoman sonycarlzeissvariosonnart1635mmf28zassm
description: www.aaronyeomanphotography.co.uk | 500px | Facebook | Twitter | Getty Images | Google+ San Giorgio Maggiore, Venice, Italy, Europe Well I have just got back from a great weekend in Venice, my first time in Venice and it was stunning, way better then I was expecting. I got a few photos but planned to just relax and take in the atmosphere. For those who have been before would probably agree with me but if you walk everywhere and explore the city there is always something different and new around every corner in Venice, its not like any other city I have been before. Very much more relaxing, probably due to the lack of cars and noisy public transport. This is the first photo I'd like to share with you all and I know its a bit of a cliche shot from Venice but I just like it so much. You should have seen the queue of photographers behind me wanting to get where I was when taking this image! Always best to get there early :-). Oh in other news, I have just bought my first ever medium format film camera, a Yashica 124G Twin Reflex Camera. Film photography is something I have wanted to get into more. The idea of having a finite amount of photos to take really appeals to me and I am planning to do a mini project with it but whether it will work or not is another thing as I think a lot of learning is required on how to get the best out of film cameras. Hope your having a great week so far. Photo Details Sony Alpha SLT-A99 / ISO100 / f/7.1 / 362s / Sony Carl Zeiss 16-35mm F2.8 ZA SSM @ 22mm / Formatt Hitech Firecrest 16 Stop Filter Software Used Lightroom CC Silver Efex Pro 2 Location Information The Grand Canal (Italian: Canal Grande, Venetian: Canałasso) is a canal in Venice, Italy. It forms one of the major water-traffic corridors in the city. Public transport is provided by water buses (Italian: vaporetti) and private water taxis, and many tourists explore the canal by gondola. At one end, the canal leads into the lagoon near the Santa Lucia railway station and the other end leads into Saint Mark Basin; in between, it makes a large reverse-S shape through the central districts (sestieri) of Venice. It is 3,800 m long, 30–90 m wide, with an average depth of five meters (16.5 ft). en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grand_Canal_(Venice)

description: ...on Ministract Island perhaps... The local farmers are very concerned about the impact it has on crops there. Trivial fact of the day - The Colorado potato beetle features in the science-fiction/horror novel The Devil's Coach Horse (also known as The Black Horde) by Richard Lewis. In the novel a plague of an evolved and flesh hungry type of the beetle is accidentally released in Great Britain and the United States. So now you know! :) ministract : the island
